description:
India Book (IB) IV,56 (ח63). Letter from Yeḥezqel b. Netanel, in Qalyūb, to his brother Ḥalfon b. Netanel, in Alexandria. The left side of the letter and the top and bottom are cut off. Yeḥezqel has been looking for a certain textile (al-ḥasīniya) on Ḥalfon's behalf, and had not found it in Fusṭāṭ. He was about to buy an inferior item from someone who, however, had just increased his price by 1.5 dīnārs; Yeḥezqel thought Ḥalfon could buy this item anyway in Alexandria. The textile artisans/merchants (al-bazzāzīn) said that the textile he sought could only be found in Cairo. The letter also mentions some tasks that Yeḥezqel is carrying out on behalf of Abū Iṣḥaq. Purchase of silk at 12 for 2 qinṭārs (recto, right margin, l. 2). Mentions Qūṣ and ʿAydhāb (l. 1), but the line is cut off.

Dated: Friday, 7 Shevaṭ [4900 AM], which is 29 December 1139 CE. Written on a reused state document in Arabic script. (Information from India Book 4.) PG
description_he: מכתב ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-12 מ-Qalyūb לאלכסנדריה. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
